content: Neural Turing Machines, which augment neural networks with external memory banks, can serve as a computational framework to formally model the anterograde amnesia experienced by the protagonist of *Memento*, where the ability to form new long-term declarative memories is lost but core procedural skills and old long-term memories remain intact. This framing also suggests a new narrative design approach for amnesia-driven thrillers, where story beats are stored in a retrievable external memory matrix that the protagonist can only access via noisy, error-prone read operations, mirroring the real narrative tension of *Memento*'s unreliable clue system.
